title: Executive Order 9264—Extension of the Provisions of Executive Order No. 9001 of December 27, 1941, to Contracts of the Department of Commerce

flags.justification: This extends 'all... functions and powers' of EO 9001 — including its broad, minimally-checked wartime contracting authority — to the Department of Commerce.
Inherits EO 9001's self-determined public-disclosure structure for contract reporting.
Single, narrow purpose: extending an existing framework to one additional department.
Inherits EO 9001's remarkably broad, open-ended definition of covered contracts.

This extension is explicitly made 'subject to the limitations and regulations contained in' EO 9001, incorporating that order's anti-corruption safeguards (profit caps, the cost-plus-percentage prohibition, the anti-lobbying warranty).
Inherits EO 9001's lack of a formal review or termination mechanism.
Inherits EO 9001's substantial bypass of normal contracting-law oversight, now extended to Commerce.
This extension is explicitly subject to EO 9001's limitations, which include the explicit preservation of labor-standards laws (Walsh-Healey, Davis-Bacon, Copeland, Eight Hour Law).
A routine, formulaic extension of an already-established framework to one additional department, not itself a novel authority claim.
A clean, single-department extension with no conflicting mandate.
Inherits EO 9001's broad exemption of covered contracts from standard government contracting-law requirements.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 6/22 = 27.3%. A second confirmed instance of structural weight inherited through incorporation by reference, alongside EO 8565's extension of EO 8389 to Romania — this time extending EO 9001's war-production contracting authority to the Department of Commerce.
